result.markdown:
--- title: GIL16 hierarchical clustering --- # Obtain rho proportionality Correlation shows how two variables vary together. In the case of RNAseq data, these correlations are inevitably relative, since we don't have the total number of reads, but rather a proportion of the total. Although algorithms like WGCNA employ Pearson correlation, this can lead to spurious associations, and more recent advances, especially in metagenomics, suggest the use of compositionally-aware methods. One such method is the rho (ρ) statistic, which can be used instead of correlation as the basis for familiar analyses and visualization methods, including coexpression networks and clustered heatmaps. In this case, we used rho proportionality to measure coexpression among GBJ002 (chromosome and pBtic235) and GIL16 genes and GIL16 samples at time points 0, 10, 30 and 60, excluding 60-mock samples, since they are not induced by MMC and may introduce noise. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} pseudocount <- min(counts[counts > 0])/10 counts <- counts + pseudocount counts <- counts[c(1:4934, 4968:5000), 25:36] all_genes <- rownames(counts) rhos <- propr(t(counts), metric = "rho") ``` ::: # Dendrogram The figure below shows a dendrogram representation of the hierarchical clustering generated by `hclust` with average method and at a distance (1 - ρ) of 0.25 (i.e., approximately 75% positive proportionality in expression). A higher cutoff height would yield larger clusters, although there might be genes with low coexpression within them. A lower cutoff height would yield greater proportionality in expression, although the clusters would be smaller. Furthermore, to simplify the analysis, only clusters with 20 or more genes were obtained. A single exception to this rule was the inclusion of a cluster with 19 genes, as it included the genes expressed from GIL16 P1-P2 tandem promoters. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} hc <- hclust(as.dist(1 - rhos@matrix), method = "average") hc_clusters <- cutree(hc, h = 0.25) cluster_sizes <- table(hc_clusters) big_ids <- c(as.numeric(names(cluster_sizes[cluster_sizes >= 20])), 470) stable_ids <- c(18, 43, 46, 128, 130, 160, 215, 255, 256, 284, 293, 333, 347, 408, 425, 727, 935) ids <- sort(c(big_ids, stable_ids)) small_ids <- as.numeric(names(cluster_sizes[cluster_sizes < 20])) small_ids <- setdiff(small_ids, c(stable_ids, 470)) leaf_order <- hc$order clusters_in_order <- hc_clusters[leaf_order] clusters_left_to_right <- rle(clusters_in_order)$values which_big <- match(ids, clusters_left_to_right) which_big <- which_big[!is.na(which_big)] x_pos <- seq_along(leaf_order) centers <- tapply(x_pos, clusters_in_order, mean) centers_big <- centers[as.character(ids)] genes_big <- names(hc_clusters)[hc_clusters %in% ids] clusters_big <- hc_clusters[hc_clusters %in% ids] cluster_gene_table <- data.frame( Cluster = clusters_big, Gene = genes_big, stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) sizes <- cluster_gene_table %>% count(Cluster, name = "Size") first_appearance <- cluster_gene_table %>% distinct(Cluster) %>% mutate(first_pos = row_number()) map_tbl <- sizes %>% left_join(first_appearance, by = "Cluster") %>% arrange(Size, first_pos) %>% group_by(Size) %>% mutate( tie_index = row_number(), letter = if (n() > 1) num_to_letters(tie_index) else rep("", n()), size_lab = sprintf("%03d", Size), new_name = paste0("GIL16_C", size_lab, letter) ) %>% ungroup() cluster_to_name <- setNames(map_tbl$new_name, map_tbl$Cluster) cluster_gene_table <- cluster_gene_table %>% mutate(Cluster = cluster_to_name[as.character(Cluster)]) %>% dplyr::left_join( annotation, by = c("Gene" = "Gene_ID") ) ids_labels <- unname(cluster_to_name[as.character(ids)]) op <- par(col = "grey", xpd = NA, mar = par("mar") + c(1,0,0,0)) plot(hc, labels = FALSE, main = NA, sub = NA, xlab = NA, ylab = "Distance (1 - ρ)") rect.hclust(hc, which = which_big, h = 0.25, border = which_big) y_min <- par("usr")[3] text(x = centers_big, y = y_min - 0.01 * diff(par("usr")[3:4]), labels = ids_labels, cex = 0.5, srt = 90, adj = 1, col = "black") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](hierarchical_clustering_gil16_files/figure-html/hc-1.png){width=1152} ::: ::: # Stability ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} clusters_ids <- sort(c(ids, small_ids)) clusters_list <- lapply(clusters_ids, function(id) { names(hc_clusters)[hc_clusters == id] }) names(clusters_list) <- clusters_ids clusters_stability <- compute_stability(clusters = clusters_ids, strain = "GIL16", frac = 0.80, B = 1000) datatable(clusters_stability, rownames = FALSE, filter = "top", extensions = c("Buttons", "Scroller"), options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"), scrollY = 400, scroller = TRUE, deferRender = TRUE)) %>% formatPercentage(columns = "Stability", digits = 2) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: # Heatmap After obtaining the dendrograms, we will represent how similar they are to each other with respect to gene expression patterns. To do this, we will use the distance of all genes in each cluster from the others to obtain an average distance (1 - ρ) and graphically represent the results in an interactive heatmap. As we can see, there is a main subdivision into two branches, among which we could distinguish some superclusters based on distance. For example, GIL16_C289 and GIL16_C061 have a distance (1 - ρ) of 0.2823, i.e., raising the threshold to this value would merge them into a supercluster. The next closest would be GIL16_C025b, which has distances of 0.3133 and 0.2728 to GIL16_C289 and GIL16_C061, respectively. Depending on the enrichment results, we could consider raising the cutoff threshold to obtain separate functions in each cluster, although for the moment we will maintain the previously defined threshold. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} labels_new <- unname(cluster_to_name[as.character(ids)]) members_list <- setNames( lapply(ids, function(k) which(hc_clusters == k)), labels_new ) avg_inter <- matrix(0, nrow = length(ids), ncol = length(ids), dimnames = list(labels_new, labels_new)) for (i in seq_len(length(ids))) { for (j in i:length(ids)) { if (i != j) { m <- mean((1 - rhos@matrix)[members_list[[i]], members_list[[j]]]) avg_inter[i, j] <- m avg_inter[j, i] <- m } } } hc_row <- hclust(as.dist(avg_inter), method = "average") hc_col <- hclust(as.dist(avg_inter), method = "average") dend_row <- as.dendrogram(hc_row) dend_col <- as.dendrogram(hc_col) dend_row_olo <- seriate_dendrogram(dend_row, as.dist(avg_inter), method = "OLO") dend_col_olo <- seriate_dendrogram(dend_col, as.dist(avg_inter), method = "OLO") order_row <- order.dendrogram(dend_row_olo) order_col <- order.dendrogram(dend_col_olo) order_x <- colnames(avg_inter)[order_col] order_y <- rownames(avg_inter)[order_row] heatmaply( avg_inter[order_y, order_x, drop = FALSE], colors = viridis(n = 256, alpha = 1, begin = 1, end = 0, option = "viridis"), distfun_row = function(x) as.dist(x), distfun_col = function(x) as.dist(x), hclustfun_row = function(d) hclust(d, method = "average"), hclustfun_col = function(d) hclust(d, method = "average"), seriate = "none", symm = TRUE, scale = "none", label_names = c("Row", "Column", "Distance (1 - ρ)"), width = 1000, height = 800 ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: # Clusters composition The interactive table below displays clusters with 20 or more genes and the genes that comprise them. Above each column is a filter box that allows to filter by cluster, gene name, location, etc. At first glance, we observe that 2207 out of 5023 genes (43.94%) are located in clusters. Also, it can be observed that a large proportion of pBtic235 genes belong to cluster GIL16_C289. Regarding the prophage region (GBJ002_00964 to GBJ002_01009 genes), some genes are in GIL16_C289, although they are more distributed than in the other hierarchical clusterings. Last but not least, GIL16 genes expressed from P1-P2 tandem promoters (plus GIL16_34, expressed from the negative strand) are found in GIL16_C019, while those expressed from P3 promoter are found in GIL16_C031. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} cluster_gene_table$Cluster <- factor(cluster_gene_table$Cluster, levels = order_x) cluster_gene_table$Element <- factor(cluster_gene_table$Element, levels = c("chromosome", "pBtic235", "GIL16")) cluster_gene_table$Strand <- factor(cluster_gene_table$Strand, levels = c("+", "-")) datatable(cluster_gene_table, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("Cluster", "Gene", "Element", "Start", "End", "Strand", "Gene symbol", "Description", "COG category", "Association", "Type", "Subtype", "Source"), filter = "top", extensions = c("Buttons", "FixedColumns", "Scroller"), options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"), fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 2), scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = 400, scroller = TRUE, deferRender = TRUE)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: # Expression plots ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} expression_plots <- clusters_expression(expression_matrix = rhos@logratio, metadata = metadata, time_points = c(0, 10, 30, 60), de_genes = c("gil16_t10_t0", "gil16_t30_t0", "gil16_t60_t0", "gil16_t60_t60-mock", "gil16_t60-mock_t0"), which_contrast = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error} ``` Error in `dplyr::select()`: ! Can't select columns that don't exist. ✖ Column `Time_points` doesn't exist. ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} expression_plots$all_clusters ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error} ``` Error: objeto 'expression_plots' no encontrado ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} expression_plots$facet_clusters ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-error} ``` Error: objeto 'expression_plots' no encontrado ``` ::: ::: # Enrichment analysis As with determining the enrichment of upregulated or downregulated genes, an enrichment analysis was performed according to the COG, KEGG, GO and EC categories to determine if clusters are enriched in any function. ## COG Regarding COG categories, 11 clusters show significant enrichment comprising 9 distinct terms. Among the most notable findings are GIL16_C071, which is enriched in genes related to cell cycle control, cell division, chromosome partitioning (**D**), and GIL16_C019, which includes 5 genes involved in amino acid transport and metabolism (**E**). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} cog_results <- cog_enrichment_by_cluster(cluster_gene_table, all_genes) datatable(cog_results, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("COG", "IN", "OUT", "Genes", "Odds ratio", "p value", "Adjusted p-value", "Cluster"), extensions = c("Buttons", "FixedColumns", "Scroller"), options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"), fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 0, rightColumns = 1), scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = 400, scroller = TRUE, deferRender = TRUE)) %>% formatRound(columns = "odds.ratio", digits = 2) %>% formatSignif(columns = c("p.value", "padj"), digits = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## KEGG Regarding KEGG terms, there is a wide variety across the clusters. GIL16_C036b is related to quorum sensing pathways, while GIL16_C027a is related to membrane transport. Regarding replication and repair pathways, we can observe two distinct clusters: GIL16_C289, associated to DNA replication, mismatch repair, homologous recombination and nucleotide excision repair, and GIL16_C050, associated to the last pathway. Interestingly, 17 clusters appears to be enriched in diverse metabolic pathways, which suggests a specialization in gene expression in the different reflected pathways. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} kegg_results <- kegg_enrichment_by_cluster(cluster_gene_table, all_genes) datatable(kegg_results, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("Category", "Subcategory", "Pathway ID", "Pathway description", "Gene ratio", "Background ratio", "Rich factor", "Fold enrichment", "Z score", "p value", "Adjusted p‑value", "q value", "Genes", "Gene count", "Cluster"), extensions = c("Buttons", "FixedColumns", "Scroller"), options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"), fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 0, rightColumns = 1), scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = 400, scroller = TRUE, deferRender = TRUE)) %>% formatRound(columns = c("RichFactor", "FoldEnrichment", "zScore"), digits = 2) %>% formatSignif(columns = c("pvalue", "p.adjust", "qvalue"), digits = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## GO GO enrichment analysis is much more complex, since the presence of few genes can result in different enrichment levels. In any case, three independent tables (BP, MF and CC) have been generated and are summarized in the Enrichment summary section. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} go_bp_results <- go_enrichment_by_cluster(cluster_gene_table, all_genes, "BP") datatable(go_bp_results, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("GO ID", "GO term description", "Gene ratio", "Background ratio", "Rich factor", "Fold enrichment", "Z score", "p value", "Adjusted p‑value", "q value", "Genes", "Gene count", "Cluster"), caption = htmltools::tags$caption( style = "caption-side: top; text-align: start;", "BP enrichment"), extensions = c("Buttons", "FixedColumns", "Scroller"), options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"), fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 0, rightColumns = 1), scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = 400, scroller = TRUE, deferRender = TRUE)) %>% formatRound(columns = c("RichFactor", "FoldEnrichment", "zScore"), digits = 2) %>% formatSignif(columns = c("pvalue", "p.adjust", "qvalue"), digits = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} go_mf_results <- go_enrichment_by_cluster(cluster_gene_table, all_genes, "MF") datatable(go_mf_results, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("GO ID", "GO term description", "Gene ratio", "Background ratio", "Rich factor", "Fold enrichment", "Z score", "p value", "Adjusted p‑value", "q value", "Genes", "Gene count", "Cluster"), caption = htmltools::tags$caption( style = "caption-side: top; text-align: start;", "MF enrichment"), extensions = c("Buttons", "FixedColumns", "Scroller"), options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"), fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 0, rightColumns = 1), scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = 400, scroller = TRUE, deferRender = TRUE)) %>% formatRound(columns = c("RichFactor", "FoldEnrichment", "zScore"), digits = 2) %>% formatSignif(columns = c("pvalue", "p.adjust", "qvalue"), digits = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} go_cc_results <- go_enrichment_by_cluster(cluster_gene_table, all_genes, "CC") datatable(go_cc_results, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("GO ID", "GO term description", "Gene ratio", "Background ratio", "Rich factor", "Fold enrichment", "Z score", "p value", "Adjusted p‑value", "q value", "Genes", "Gene count", "Cluster"), caption = htmltools::tags$caption( style = "caption-side: top; text-align: start;", "CC enrichment"), extensions = c("Buttons", "FixedColumns", "Scroller"), options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"), fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 0, rightColumns = 1), scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = 400, scroller = TRUE, deferRender = TRUE)) %>% formatRound(columns = c("RichFactor", "FoldEnrichment", "zScore"), digits = 2) %>% formatSignif(columns = c("pvalue", "p.adjust", "qvalue"), digits = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## EC Although there are not many EC terms, some interesting results can be observed. For example, GIL16_C050 is enriched at each of numbers **7**, **7.1** and **7.1.2**, which correspond to some F0F1 ATP synthase subunits. Interestingly, other subunits of this enzyme are found in GIL16_C100, as shown in the cluster composition table. In fact, there are other genes not annotated by EC, since this cluster contains up to 6 subunits genes of this enzyme. Another enriched cluster at all three levels is GIL16_C037, specifically at numbers **6**, **6.1** and **6.1.1** by the presence of tRNA ligases genes. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} ec_1_results <- ec_enrichment_by_cluster(cluster_gene_table, all_genes, 1) datatable(ec_1_results, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("EC number", "Class", "IN", "OUT", "Genes", "Odds ratio", "p value", "Adjusted p-value", "Cluster"), caption = htmltools::tags$caption( style = "caption-side: top; text-align: start;", "EC level 1 enrichment"), extensions = "Buttons", options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"))) %>% formatRound(columns = "odds.ratio", digits = 2) %>% formatSignif(columns = c("p.value", "padj"), digits = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} ec_2_results <- ec_enrichment_by_cluster(cluster_gene_table, all_genes, 2) datatable(ec_2_results, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("EC number", "Class", "Subclass", "IN", "OUT", "Genes", "Odds ratio", "p value", "Adjusted p-value", "Cluster"), caption = htmltools::tags$caption( style = "caption-side: top; text-align: start;", "EC level 2 enrichment"), extensions = "Buttons", options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"))) %>% formatRound(columns = "odds.ratio", digits = 2) %>% formatSignif(columns = c("p.value", "padj"), digits = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} ec_3_results <- ec_enrichment_by_cluster(cluster_gene_table, all_genes, 3) datatable(ec_3_results, rownames = FALSE, colnames = c("EC number", "Class", "Subclass", "Sub-subclass", "IN", "OUT", "Genes", "Odds ratio", "p value", "Adjusted p-value", "Cluster"), caption = htmltools::tags$caption( style = "caption-side: top; text-align: start;", "EC level 3 enrichment"), extensions = "Buttons", options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"))) %>% formatRound(columns = "odds.ratio", digits = 2) %>% formatSignif(columns = c("p.value", "padj"), digits = 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: # Enrichment summary This table contains a summary of the enrichments for each category. The subcategory was used for KEGG and the description for GO. Although you can scroll, I recommend exporting using the Excel button and viewing the table in full screen. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} size_df <- as.data.frame(table(cluster_gene_table$Cluster)) %>% dplyr::rename(Cluster = Var1, Size = Freq) cog_df <- cog_results %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% dplyr::summarise(COG = collapse_vals(COG), .groups = "drop") kegg_df <- kegg_results %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% dplyr::summarise(KEGG = collapse_vals(subcategory), .groups = "drop") go_bp_df <- go_bp_results %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% dplyr::summarise(`GO-BP`= collapse_vals(Description), .groups = "drop") go_mf_df <- go_mf_results %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% dplyr::summarise(`GO-MF`= collapse_vals(Description), .groups = "drop") go_cc_df <- go_cc_results %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% dplyr::summarise(`GO-CC`= collapse_vals(Description), .groups = "drop") ec_1_df <- ec_1_results %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% dplyr::summarise(`EC-1` = collapse_vals(EC), .groups = "drop") ec_2_df <- ec_2_results %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% dplyr::summarise(`EC-2` = collapse_vals(EC), .groups = "drop") ec_3_df <- ec_3_results %>% group_by(Cluster) %>% dplyr::summarise(`EC-3` = collapse_vals(EC), .groups = "drop") summary_df <- size_df %>% dplyr::left_join(cog_df, by = "Cluster") %>% dplyr::left_join(kegg_df, by = "Cluster") %>% dplyr::left_join(go_bp_df, by = "Cluster") %>% dplyr::left_join(go_mf_df, by = "Cluster") %>% dplyr::left_join(go_cc_df, by = "Cluster") %>% dplyr::left_join(ec_1_df, by = "Cluster") %>% dplyr::left_join(ec_2_df, by = "Cluster") %>% dplyr::left_join(ec_3_df, by = "Cluster") %>% dplyr::select(Cluster, Size, COG, KEGG, `GO-BP`, `GO-MF`, `GO-CC`, `EC-1`, `EC-2`, `EC-3`) %>% dplyr::mutate(Cluster = factor(Cluster, levels = levels(cluster_gene_table$Cluster))) datatable(summary_df, rownames = FALSE, filter = "top", extensions = c("Buttons", "FixedColumns", "Scroller"), options = list( autoWidth = TRUE, dom = "Brftip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"), fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 2), scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = 400, scroller = TRUE, deferRender = TRUE)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: # Save results ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} gil16_clusters <- split(cluster_gene_table$Gene, cluster_gene_table$Cluster) saveRDS(gil16_clusters, file = "gil16_clusters.rds") saveRDS(clusters_stability, file = "gil16_clusters_stability.rds") ``` :::
